mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects increasing manufacturing output and supply-side strength, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month change and subsequent 20-day SPX return is below 0.05 over any rolling 2-year window, or sign accuracy < 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 4.5%, the labor market becomes tight, triggering wage inflation pressures that compress profit margins and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE drops below 4.5%, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is positive more than 55% of the time, or average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals broad-based demand growth, driving higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal (both series rising month-over-month) fails to produce a positive median forward return over 20 days, or the average return is below the unconditional median across all available samples.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound environment (FEDFUNDS at or below 0.25%), rising CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) reduces real interest rates and signals reflation, which is positive for equity valuations and S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%, the correlation between CPIAUCSL 3-month change and forward 20-day SPX return is negative or insignificant (|rank IC| < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The 3-month rolling change in PAYEMS has a Rank IC below 0.04 with subsequent 60-day SPX returns over any 3-year walk-forward window, or directional accuracy < 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) captures the broadest measure of economic output; rising GDP supports corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The quarter-over-quarter change in GDPC1 has a Rank IC below 0.05 with subsequent 60-day SPX return over any 5-year walk-forward window, or the sign of the relationship is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is near zero and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising, the implied real interest rate declines, lowering the discount rate on equities and boosting S&P 500 prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS * CPIAUCSL) or the implied real rate change fails to produce a Rank IC above 0.03 with forward 20-day SPX returns over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer demand and immediate revenue for retailers and producers, positively affecting S&P 500 returns over a short weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The week-over-week change in RSAFS has a Rank IC below 0.02 with forward 5-day SPX returns over any 1-year walk-forward window, or the median return following positive weeks is not above unconditional median.
